$comment: Base culture. Every key the app uses lives here; other cultures override per-key and fall back to this.

player.$comment.songs: ICU plural — en uses one/other; =0 is an explicit exact-match branch that wins over the category.
player.songs: {count, plural, =0 {No songs} one {# song} other {# songs}}
player.songsBy: {count, plural, =0 {No songs by {artist}} one {# song by {artist}} other {# songs by {artist}}}

profile.$comment.invited: ICU select on gender (male/female/other), with a nested {name} placeholder inside each branch.
profile.invited: {gender, select, male {He invited {name}} female {She invited {name}} other {They invited {name}}}

files.$comment: Polish proves per-language four-form plurals (one/few/many) — en-US here only has the one/other shape.
files.count: {count, plural, =0 {No files} one {# file} other {# files}}

validation.$comment: Native form-validation messages (form-validation.md). Rule factories default to these keys; an app may pass its own. Resolved at the bound error TextEl — culture-reactive, zero-alloc on a hit.
